rulings.Case Title: LIZA SWIFT VS. LARRY SWIFT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Renewed Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Substitute Plaintiff Janesta Downey, Trustee Of The Liza Denise Swift Living Trust, In Place Of Deceased Plaintiff Liza Swift
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF LIZA SWIFT's Renewed Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Substitute Plaintiff Janesta Downey, Trustee Of The Liza Denise Swift Living Trust, In Place Of Deceased Plaintiff Liza Swift Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (prevailing party to prepare order in conformity with the tentative ruling in additions to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). Plaintiff's Motion to Substitute Plaintiff. Plaintiff's Motion to Substitute Plaintiff is GRANTED. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SHOREVIEW APARTMENTS, LP VS. DEANNA RUSSELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT DEANNA BELFREY-RUSSELL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. rulings.Case Title: JASMINE LEE-ELLIOTT VS. JOHN CHRISTIAN VOLDAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery; To Stay Case Pending Plaintiff'S Response To Discovery Requests; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2020 line 6. DEFENDANT JOHN VOLDAL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery; To Stay Case Pending Plaintiff'S Response To Discovery Requests; And For Monetary Sanctions Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (prevailing party to prepare order in conformity with the tentative ruling in additions to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Discovery is OFF CALENDAR. This motion was submitted for filing during the stay under this Court's General Orders. No exemption order was sought or granted before or after the filing. The Court notes however that the parties voluntarily meet and confer efforts during the stay appear to be incomplete and urges the parties to continue such efforts as the stay has now been lifted. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DAVID DILLON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash And Motion To Quash; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Hannah Flanery In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 22, 2020 line 9. DEFENDANT DAVID DILLON's Motion To Quash Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (prevailing party to prepare order in conformity with the tentative ruling in additions to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). Defendant's Motion to Quash is DENIED. Defendant's motion is based in its entirety on Court's May 18, 2020 decision. No other basis to challenge the Court's jurisdiction in this action has been stated. This is an improper motion for reconsideration. Even if the motion for reconsideration were properly and timely presented, Defendant provides no basis for the Court to change its ruling. The Court considered competing declarations and legal argument presented in wiring and orally by the parties, and made a determination on the record in a written order in its discretion that the action is necessary to protect public health and safety without making factual determinations reserved for the jury (or the trial judge) regarding the events forming the basis of the operative Notice to Quit in this action. = (501/CFH)
